conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you some questions.

Let me start with the first question:

**Do you have any children, and if so, how many children are you asking about?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I have one child. I'm interested in finding out if I'm eligible to claim Child Benefit for them.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. I'll need to gather some more details to assess your eligibility.

**How old is your child?**

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you. Since your child is 18, I need to understand their current situation, as eligibility can depend on whether they're in education or training.

**Is your 18-year-old child currently in approved education or training?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, they are. They're studying for their A-levels at sixth form college.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
That's helpful information. Now I need to understand your living situation and relationship with your child.

**Does your child live with you?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, they live at home with me.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you. Now I need to understand if you're responsible for your child.

**Are you responsible for your child? This means you're the one who looks after them and pays for their upbringing.**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I am. I'm responsible for them and pay for their upbringing.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you. I have a few more questions to complete the assessment.

**Are you currently living in the UK?**

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Excellent. Just a couple more questions to complete the assessment.

**Has anyone else already claimed Child Benefit for this child?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for them.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you. One final question:

**What is your child's name?** (I need this for the eligibility assessment)

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
## Good news! You are eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.

**Here's why:**

Alex meets the eligibility criteria because:
- They are 18 years old and studying for A-levels at sixth form college, which counts as approved education
- Under Child Benefit rules, you can claim for children aged 16-19 who are in approved education or training
- Alex lives with you, and you're responsible for them
- You live in the UK
- No one else is currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex

You should be able to successfully apply for Child Benefit. The benefit can continue until Alex turns 20, or until they leave approved education or training - whichever comes first.

Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit?

claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 18 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 18 and in approved education
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 18 and in approved education; Child lives with claimant; Child is not in local authority care; Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child is 18 and in approved education
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

child_evaluations.reasoning: Alex is 18 years old and is studying for A-levels at sixth form college, which qualifies as approved education. According to UK Child Benefit rules, you can claim Child Benefit for a child aged 16-19 if they stay in approved education or training. Alex lives with the claimant, who is responsible for them and pays for their upbringing. The claimant lives in the UK (Manchester), and no one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex. All eligibility criteria are met: the child is under 20 and in approved education, lives with the claimant who is responsible for them, the claimant is in the UK, and there is no duplicate claim.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant has one child (Alex, age 18) who is studying A-levels at sixth form college. The claimant is responsible for Alex, who lives with them in Manchester, UK. No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex. Since Alex is 18 and in approved education (A-levels), they meet the extended eligibility criteria for children aged 16-19 in education or training. The claimant meets all the requirements for Child Benefit eligibility.
